title: Sutton SignWriting and Formal SignWriting Through the Framework
subtitle: A self-application of the review method to the writing system and its technical model
summary: A framework-based analysis distinguishing SignWriting as the writing system from Formal SignWriting as its technical text model.
abstract: This framework application evaluates Sutton SignWriting and Formal SignWriting with the same criteria used elsewhere in the critical review series. It distinguishes SignWriting as the human writing system from Formal SignWriting as the technical text model that preserves written signs for storage, search, processing, and rendering. The analysis concludes that the combined system is strongest where the framework asks about durable signed text, structured written units, and infrastructure viability, while remaining careful about adoption, literacy outcomes, handwriting practice, and unresolved standards questions. Its central claim is disciplined: SignWriting and Formal SignWriting establish a coherent full-path model for plane-based signed writing, but they do not settle every educational, empirical, or institutional question by themselves.
synopsis: This entry applies the review framework to SignWriting and Formal SignWriting themselves. It uses the framework not only against external literature, but also against the system it is meant to clarify. The result is a sharper distinction between SignWriting as a writing system and Formal SignWriting as a text model, along with a clearer statement of what the combined system does and does not yet establish.

slides.title: Self-Application
slides.summary: The framework is applied to SignWriting and Formal SignWriting themselves, not only to external sources.
bullets: Distinguish writing system from text model
slides.speech: This entry applies the review framework to SignWriting and Formal SignWriting themselves. The point is methodological fairness: the same distinctions used for external literature should also clarify the platform's own claims.

image_prompts.concrete: Self-application review slide showing SignWriting and Formal SignWriting evaluated by the same framework used for external literature
image_prompts.abstract: A mirror held up to a framework, showing methodological fairness

slides.title: Writing And Text Model
slides.summary: SignWriting is the human writing system; Formal SignWriting is the technical text model that preserves written signs for software.
slides.speech: The central distinction is between SignWriting as the human writing system and Formal SignWriting as the technical text model. FSW and SWU are encoding strategies inside that technical layer, while rendering and search are downstream.
slides.notes: This is the most important clarification.

image_prompts.concrete: Layered slide distinguishing SignWriting as a human writing system from Formal SignWriting as a technical text model with FSW and SWU underneath
image_prompts.abstract: Human writing and machine-readable text model aligned but distinct

slides.title: Strengths And Limits
slides.summary: The combined system is strongest for durable signed text and structured written units, while adoption, literacy outcomes, handwriting, and standards questions remain open.
slides.speech: The combined system is strongest where the framework asks about durable signed text, structured written units, and infrastructure viability. It should remain careful about adoption, literacy outcomes, handwriting practice, and unresolved standards questions.

image_prompts.concrete: Balanced assessment slide showing strong durable signed text model on one side and open adoption, literacy, handwriting, and standards questions on the other
image_prompts.abstract: A strong central structure with clear open boundaries around it

slides.title: Disciplined Claim
slides.summary: SignWriting and Formal SignWriting establish a coherent full-path model, but they do not settle every educational, empirical, or institutional question.
slides.speech: The disciplined claim is that SignWriting and Formal SignWriting establish a coherent full-path model for plane-based signed writing. They do not, by themselves, settle every educational, empirical, or institutional question.

image_prompts.concrete: Disciplined claim slide showing a coherent full-path model for plane-based signed writing with separate open questions clearly marked
image_prompts.abstract: Bounded confidence: a complete path with visible research frontiers
